Title: Riboflavin integrates cellular energetics and cell cycle to regulate maize seed development

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced SDH1 flavinylation in o18 lowers complex II activity and contributes to impaired energy metabolism downstream of riboflavin deficiency.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in-gel fluorometry flavinylation assay; immunoblot; complex II activity assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDH1 flavinylation was strongly decreased in o18 mutant endosperm. 2. SDH1 abundance and complex II activity were reduced under riboflavin/FAD deficiency. 3. SDH1 acts as a downstream flavoprotein linking riboflavin supply to mitochondrial respiration.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SDH1 is not the causal gene of the mutant but a core experimentally examined downstream flavoprotein mediating nutrient-metabolism effects.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing endosperm at 15–18 DAP; riboflavin deficiency caused by o18 mutation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protein level of CDKA;1 is reduced in o18, associating riboflavin-dependent energy status with G1/S progression and endoreduplication.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: immunoblot; flow cytometry; qRT-PCR of cell-cycle genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CDKA;1 protein was decreased in o18 endosperm. 2. Reduced CDKA;1 correlates with G1 and S-phase arrest and impaired endoreduplication. 3. CDKA;1 is part of the downstream cell-cycle module affected by riboflavin deficiency.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated here as a common downstream effector rather than a primary regulatory gene for nutrient metabolism.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing endosperm at 10–15 DAP; riboflavin deficiency caused by o18 mutation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RBR1 protein level is reduced in o18, linking riboflavin-dependent energetics to endosperm cell-cycle control.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBR1 abundance was reduced in o18 mutant kernels. 2. Reduced RBR1 coincided with altered expression of DNA replication genes and defective cell-cycle progression. 3. RBR1 functions downstream of riboflavin-dependent energetic control in endosperm development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the focal causal gene, but directly assayed as a major downstream node in the metabolic phenotype.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing endosperm at 10–15 DAP; riboflavin deficiency caused by o18 mutation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely impaired under FAD deficiency in o18, causing increased H3K4me3 and induction of cell-cycle genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: H3K4me3 immunoblot; ChIP-qPCR; inference from FAD dependence and expression analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The paper proposes maize LSD1-like demethylases as FAD-dependent effectors of riboflavin status. 2. H3K4me3 increased in o18 kernels, consistent with reduced LSD1-like activity. 3. This provides an epigenetic link between vitamin B2 metabolism and cell-cycle gene expression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic involvement is inferred rather than directly proven by mutant analysis of specific maize LSD1-like genes.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing endosperm at 15 DAP; FAD deficiency in o18

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W64A/W22/B73 background used in different assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W64A introgression background; KN5585 used for CRISPR lines
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: maize UBQ promoter for overexpression construct UBQ::O18 in maize; 35S promoter for O18-GFP in localization/Arabidopsis overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of FMN and FAD cofactors required for mitochondrial respiration, cellular energetics, seed development, and osmotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing seed endosperm; plastids/chloroplast-containing tissues for biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: initial committed entry reactions of de novo riboflavin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RibA/RibB bifunctional enzyme with GCH II (PF00925) and DHBPS (PF00926) domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: constitutively expressed in many tissues; high in developing leaves; expressed in developing kernels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: developing endosperm and other tissues; constitutive expression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Initiates de novo riboflavin biosynthesis by converting GTP via GCH II activity and ribulose-5-phosphate via DHBPS activity to the two precursor branches required for riboflavin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; committed initial step; rate-limiting/key control step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate precursor branches and is required for direct biosynthesis of riboflavin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Yes; explicitly described as a rate-limiting bifunctional enzyme and key initiator of riboflavin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seeds; riboflavin feeding; mannitol osmotic stress; NaCl stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq differential expression analysis in endosperm at 18 DAP; public maize RNA-seq expression datasets from MaizeGDB; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; allelism tests of o18-1/o18-2/o18-3; CRISPR mutagenesis; yeast and E. coli complementation; subcellular localization; LC-MS/MS quantification of riboflavin/FMN/FAD; embryo rescue with riboflavin; overexpression in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes riboflavin biosynthesis; loss of function nearly abolishes riboflavin, FMN and FAD accumulation, disrupts mitochondrial respiration and seed filling, and increases lysine; overexpression increases riboflavin and osmotic tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. O18 encodes ZmRIBA1, the only active bifunctional RIBA enzyme in maize. 2. O18 is plastid-localized and catalyzes the first committed reactions of riboflavin biosynthesis. 3. o18 mutation reduces riboflavin to ~3% of WT and strongly decreases FMN/FAD. 4. Riboflavin deficiency impairs mitochondrial complex I/II, shifts metabolism toward glycolysis, and arrests endosperm cell cycle. 5. O18 overexpression increases riboflavin accumulation and improves osmotic tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for biofortification of vitamin B2 and for engineering osmotic stress tolerance; however, complete loss-of-function causes severe seed lethality and yield penalty, so partial upregulation or transgenic overexpression is preferable. Could be used for metabolic engineering in seed and stress-resilience programs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes embryo lethality, reduced kernel weight, reduced starch, impaired endosperm filling, and chlorotic rescued seedlings. Overexpression improved osmotic tolerance but not salt tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residue G185 is essential for DHBPS activity in maize O18. O18 mutation also indirectly raises lysine due to reduced zein accumulation. The paper names the gene O18 and protein ZmRIBA1.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: precursor of FMN/FAD and required for normal seed development
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: initial committed entry reactions of de novo riboflavin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: riboflavin precursors via GCH II and DHBPS reactions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the initial bifunctional steps of riboflavin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for direct biosynthesis of riboflavin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the only active bifunctional enzyme in Arabidopsis in the context discussed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout and seed-abortion phenotype analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AtRIBA1 causes aborted seeds, supporting a conserved requirement of RIBA-mediated riboflavin synthesis for seed development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout of AtRIBA1 produced aborted seeds in heterozygotes. 2. This supports a conserved role of RIBA genes in seed development across monocots and dicots. 3. AtRIBA1 serves as comparative evidence for the maize O18 findings.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indicates conserved essentiality, cautioning against knockout strategies in dicots; useful for comparative metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is directly experimentally manipulated in Results, though mainly as cross-species validation rather than the main maize target.
